1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from typing import Callable
4	
5	from noosphere.models import CascadeEdgeRelation, Method
6	
7	
8	class MethodCollisionError(Exception):
9	    pass
10	
11	
12	class MethodNotFoundError(Exception):
13	    pass
14	
15	
16	def _parse_semver(version: str) -> tuple[int, ...]:
17	    parts: list[int] = []
18	    for p in version.split("."):
19	        try:
20	            parts.append(int(p))
21	        except ValueError:
22	            parts.append(0)
23	    return tuple(parts)
24	
25	
26	class MethodRegistry:
27	    def __init__(self) -> None:
28	        self._specs: dict[tuple[str, str], Method] = {}
29	        self._fns: dict[tuple[str, str], Callable] = {}
30	        self._emits_edges: dict[str, list[CascadeEdgeRelation]] = {}
31	
32	    def register(self, spec: Method, fn: Callable) -> None:
33	        key = (spec.name, spec.version)
34	        if key in self._specs:
35	            raise MethodCollisionError(
36	                f"Method {spec.name} v{spec.version} already registered"
37	            )
38	        self._specs[key] = spec
39	        self._fns[key] = fn
40	
41	    def set_emits_edges(
42	        self, method_id: str, edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation]
43	    ) -> None:
44	        self._emits_edges[method_id] = list(edges)
45	
46	    def get_emits_edges(self, method_id: str) -> list[CascadeEdgeRelation]:
47	        return self._emits_edges.get(method_id, [])
48	
49	    def get(
50	        self,
51	        name: str,
52	        version: str = "latest",
53	        include_retired: bool = False,
54	    ) -> tuple[Method, Callable]:
55	        if version != "latest":
56	            key = (name, version)
57	            if key not in self._specs:
58	                raise MethodNotFoundError(
59	                    f"Method {name} v{version} not found"
60	                )
61	            spec = self._specs[key]
62	            if spec.status == "retired" and not include_retired:
63	                raise MethodNotFoundError(
64	                    f"Method {name} v{version} is retired"
65	                )
66	            return spec, self._fns[key]
67	
68	        candidates: list[tuple[str, Method]] = []
69	        for (n, v), spec in self._specs.items():
70	            if n != name:
71	                continue
72	            if spec.status == "retired" and not include_retired:
73	                continue
74	            candidates.append((v, spec))
75	
76	        if not candidates:
77	            raise MethodNotFoundError(f"No active method found for {name}")
78	
79	        candidates.sort(key=lambda x: _parse_semver(x[0]), reverse=True)
80	        best_version = candidates[0][0]
81	        return self._specs[(name, best_version)], self._fns[(name, best_version)]
82	
83	    def list(self, status_filter: str | None = None) -> list[Method]:
84	        specs = list(self._specs.values())
85	        if status_filter is not None:
86	            specs = [s for s in specs if s.status == status_filter]
87	        return specs
88	
89	
90	REGISTRY = MethodRegistry()
91

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import contextvars
4	import hashlib
5	import json
6	import logging
7	import os
8	import subprocess
9	from datetime import datetime, timezone
10	from functools import wraps
11	from typing import Any, Callable
12	from uuid import uuid4
13	
14	from pydantic import BaseModel
15	
16	from noosphere.models import (
17	    CascadeEdgeRelation,
18	    Method,
19	    MethodImplRef,
20	    MethodInvocation,
21	    MethodType,
22	)
23	from noosphere.methods._hooks import _FAILURE_HOOKS, _POST_HOOKS, _PRE_HOOKS
24	from noosphere.methods._registry import REGISTRY
25	
26	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
27	
28	CORRELATION_ID: contextvars.ContextVar[str] = contextvars.ContextVar(
29	    "CORRELATION_ID", default=""
30	)
31	TENANT_ID: contextvars.ContextVar[str] = contextvars.ContextVar(
32	    "TENANT_ID", default="default"
33	)
34	
35	_store_factory: Callable[..., Any] | None = None
36	
37	
38	def set_store_factory(factory: Callable[..., Any]) -> None:
39	    global _store_factory
40	    _store_factory = factory
41	
42	
43	def _get_store() -> Any:
44	    if _store_factory is not None:
45	        try:
46	            return _store_factory()
47	        except Exception:
48	            return None
49	    return None
50	
51	
52	def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
53	    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
54	        obj = obj.model_dump(mode="json")
55	    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)
56	
57	
58	def _current_git_sha() -> str:
59	    sha = os.environ.get("THESEUS_GIT_SHA")
60	    if sha:
61	        return sha
62	    try:
63	        result = subprocess.run(
64	            ["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
65	            capture_output=True,
66	            text=True,
67	            timeout=5,
68	        )
69	        if result.returncode == 0:
70	            return result.stdout.strip()
71	    except Exception:
72	        pass
73	    return "unknown"
74	
75	
76	def _current_correlation_id() -> str:
77	    return CORRELATION_ID.get()
78	
79	
80	def _current_tenant_id() -> str:
81	    return TENANT_ID.get()
82	
83	
84	def _validate_against_schema(schema: Any, data: Any) -> Any:
85	    if isinstance(schema, type) and issubclass(schema, BaseModel):
86	        if isinstance(data, schema):
87	            return data
88	        return schema.model_validate(data)
89	    if isinstance(schema, dict) and schema:
90	        try:
91	            import jsonschema as _js
92	
93	            _js.validate(data, schema)
94	        except ImportError:
95	            pass
96	    return data
97	
98	
99	def _utcnow() -> datetime:
100	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
101	
102	
103	def _run_failure_path(
104	    spec: Method,
105	    inv: MethodInvocation,
106	    validated_input: Any,
107	    exc: BaseException,
108	) -> None:
109	    inv = inv.model_copy(
110	        update={
111	            "ended_at": _utcnow(),
112	            "succeeded": False,
113	            "error_kind": type(exc).__name__,
114	        }
115	    )
116	    for hook_name, hook in _FAILURE_HOOKS:
117	        try:
118	            hook(spec, inv, validated_input, exc)
119	        except Exception:
120	            logger.exception("Failure hook %s raised", hook_name)
121	    store = _get_store()
122	    if store is not None:
123	        try:
124	            store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
125	        except Exception:
126	            logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
127	
128	
129	def register_method(
130	    *,
131	    name: str,
132	    version: str,
133	    method_type: MethodType,
134	    input_schema: Any,
135	    output_schema: Any,
136	    description: str,
137	    rationale: str,
138	    preconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
139	    postconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
140	    dependencies: list[tuple[str, str]] | tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (),
141	    owner: str,
142	    status: str = "active",
143	    nondeterministic: bool = False,
144	    emits_edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation] | tuple[CascadeEdgeRelation, ...] = (),
145	) -> Callable:
146	    def decorator(fn: Callable) -> Callable:
147	        if isinstance(input_schema, type) and issubclass(input_schema, BaseModel):
148	            in_schema_dict = input_schema.model_json_schema()
149	        elif isinstance(input_schema, dict):
150	            in_schema_dict = input_schema
151	        else:
152	            in_schema_dict = {}
153	
154	        if isinstance(output_schema, type) and issubclass(output_schema, BaseModel):
155	            out_schema_dict = output_schema.model_json_schema()
156	        elif isinstance(output_schema, dict):
157	            out_schema_dict = output_schema
158	        else:
159	            out_schema_dict = {}
160	
161	        impl = MethodImplRef(
162	            module=fn.__module__,
163	            fn_name=fn.__qualname__,
164	            git_sha=_current_git_sha(),
165	            image_digest=None,
166	        )
167	
168	        spec_for_hash = {
169	            "name": name,
170	            "version": version,
171	            "method_type": method_type.value,
172	            "input_schema": in_schema_dict,
173	            "output_schema": out_schema_dict,
174	            "description": description,
175	            "rationale": rationale,
176	            "preconditions": list(preconditions),
177	            "postconditions": list(postconditions),
178	            "dependencies": [list(d) for d in dependencies],
179	            "implementation": impl.model_dump(mode="json"),
180	            "owner": owner,
181	            "status": status,
182	            "nondeterministic": nondeterministic,
183	        }
184	
185	        method_id = hashlib.sha256(
186	            _canonical_json(spec_for_hash).encode()
187	        ).hexdigest()[:32]
188	
189	        spec = Method(
190	            method_id=method_id,
191	            name=name,
192	            version=version,
193	            method_type=method_type,
194	            input_schema=in_schema_dict,
195	            output_schema=out_schema_dict,
196	            description=description,
197	            rationale=rationale,
198	            preconditions=list(preconditions),
199	            postconditions=list(postconditions),
200	            dependencies=list(dependencies),
201	            implementation=impl,
202	            owner=owner,
203	            status=status,
204	            nondeterministic=nondeterministic,
205	            created_at=_utcnow(),
206	        )
207	
208	        @wraps(fn)
209	        def wrapped(input_data: Any) -> Any:
210	            validated_input = _validate_against_schema(input_schema, input_data)
211	            input_hash = hashlib.sha256(
212	                _canonical_json(validated_input).encode()
213	            ).hexdigest()
214	
215	            inv = MethodInvocation(
216	                id=str(uuid4()),
217	                method_id=spec.method_id,
218	                input_hash=input_hash,
219	                output_hash="",
220	                started_at=_utcnow(),
221	                ended_at=None,
222	                succeeded=False,
223	                error_kind=None,
224	                correlation_id=_current_correlation_id(),
225	                tenant_id=_current_tenant_id(),
226	            )
227	
228	            try:
229	                for _hook_name, hook in _PRE_HOOKS:
230	                    hook(spec, inv, validated_input)
231	            except Exception as e:
232	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
233	                raise
234	
235	            try:
236	                result = fn(validated_input)
237	                _validate_against_schema(output_schema, result)
238	                output_hash = hashlib.sha256(
239	                    _canonical_json(result).encode()
240	                ).hexdigest()
241	                inv = inv.model_copy(
242	                    update={
243	                        "ended_at": _utcnow(),
244	                        "succeeded": True,
245	                        "output_hash": output_hash,
246	                    }
247	                )
248	
249	                for hook_name, hook in _POST_HOOKS:
250	                    try:
251	                        hook(spec, inv, validated_input, result)
252	                    except Exception:
253	                        logger.exception("Post-hook %s raised", hook_name)
254	
255	                store = _get_store()
256	                if store is not None:
257	                    try:
258	                        store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
259	                    except Exception:
260	                        logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
261	
262	                return result
263	            except Exception as e:
264	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
265	                raise
266	
267	        wrapped.__method_spec__ = spec  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
268	
269	        REGISTRY.register(spec, wrapped)
270	
271	        if emits_edges:
272	            REGISTRY.set_emits_edges(method_id, list(emits_edges))
273	
274	        store = _get_store()
275	        if store is not None:
276	            try:
277	                store.insert_method(spec)
278	            except Exception:
279	                logger.exception("Failed to persist method spec")
280	
281	        return wrapped
282	
283	    return decorator
284

39	]

from noosphere.methods._decorator import (
    CORRELATION_ID,
    TENANT_ID,
    register_method,
    set_store_factory,
)
from noosphere.methods._hooks import (
    register_failure_hook,
    register_post_hook,
    register_pre_hook,
    unregister_hook,
)
from noosphere.methods._registry import (
    REGISTRY,
    MethodCollisionError,
    MethodNotFoundError,
)


def get_method(
    name: str, version: str = "latest", include_retired: bool = False
):
    return REGISTRY.get(name, version=version, include_retired=include_retired)


__all__ = [
    "CORRELATION_ID",
    "REGISTRY",
    "TENANT_ID",
    "MethodCollisionError",
    "MethodNotFoundError",
    "get_method",
    "register_failure_hook",
    "register_method",
    "register_post_hook",
    "register_pre_hook",
    "set_store_factory",
    "unregister_hook",
]

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import logging
4	from collections import deque
5	from datetime import datetime, timezone
6	from typing import Optional
7	from uuid import uuid4
8	
9	from noosphere.models import (
10	    CascadeEdge,
11	    CascadeEdgeRelation,
12	    CascadeNode,
13	    CascadeNodeKind,
14	)
15	
16	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
17	
18	
19	class CascadeCycleError(Exception):
20	    """Adding this edge would create a cycle in the depends_on subgraph."""
21	
22	
23	class CascadeGraph:
24	    def __init__(self, store) -> None:  # noqa: ANN001
25	        self._store = store
26	
27	    # ── node helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28	
29	    def add_node(
30	        self,
31	        *,
32	        kind: CascadeNodeKind,
33	        ref: str,
34	        attrs: Optional[dict] = None,
35	        node_id: Optional[str] = None,
36	    ) -> str:
37	        nid = node_id or str(uuid4())
38	        node = CascadeNode(
39	            node_id=nid,
40	            kind=kind,
41	            ref=ref,
42	            attrs=attrs or {},
43	        )
44	        self._store.insert_cascade_node(node)
45	        return nid
46	
47	    # ── edge helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
48	
49	    def add_edge(
50	        self,
51	        *,
52	        src: str,
53	        dst: str,
54	        relation: CascadeEdgeRelation,
55	        method_invocation_id: str,
56	        confidence: float,
57	        unresolved: bool = False,
58	    ) -> str:
59	        from noosphere.store import CascadeEdgeConflictError, CascadeEdgeOrphanError
60	
61	        inv = self._store.get_method_invocation(method_invocation_id)
62	        if inv is None:
63	            raise CascadeEdgeOrphanError(
64	                f"method_invocation_id {method_invocation_id!r} not found"
65	            )
66	
67	        if relation in (CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS, CascadeEdgeRelation.REFUTES):
68	            opposite = (
69	                CascadeEdgeRelation.REFUTES
70	                if relation == CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS
71	                else CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS
72	            )
73	            for existing in self._store.iter_cascade_edges(
74	                src=src, dst=dst, relation=opposite.value, include_retracted=False
75	            ):
76	                raise CascadeEdgeConflictError(
77	                    f"Non-retracted {opposite.value} edge exists "
78	                    f"between {src} -> {dst}"
79	                )
80	
81	        if relation == CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON:
82	            self._check_cycle(src, dst)
83	
84	        edge_id = str(uuid4())
85	        edge = CascadeEdge(
86	            edge_id=edge_id,
87	            src=src,
88	            dst=dst,
89	            relation=relation,
90	            method_invocation_id=method_invocation_id,
91	            confidence=confidence,
92	            unresolved=unresolved,
93	            established_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
94	        )
95	        self._store.insert_cascade_edge(edge)
96	        return edge_id
97	
98	    def retract_edge(self, edge_id: str) -> None:
99	        self._store.retract_cascade_edge(edge_id, datetime.now(timezone.utc))
100	
101	    def iter_edges(
102	        self,
103	        *,
104	        src: Optional[str] = None,
105	        dst: Optional[str] = None,
106	        relation: Optional[str] = None,
107	        include_retracted: bool = False,
108	    ):
109	        yield from self._store.iter_cascade_edges(
110	            src=src, dst=dst, relation=relation, include_retracted=include_retracted
111	        )
112	
113	    # ── cycle detection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
114	
115	    def _check_cycle(self, src: str, dst: str) -> None:
116	        """BFS forward from dst in depends_on subgraph; if we reach src it's a cycle."""
117	        visited: set[str] = set()
118	        queue: deque[str] = deque([dst])
119	        while queue:
120	            current = queue.popleft()
121	            if current == src:
122	                raise CascadeCycleError(
123	                    f"Adding depends_on edge {src} -> {dst} would create a cycle"
124	                )
125	            if current in visited:
126	                continue
127	            visited.add(current)
128	            for edge in self._store.iter_cascade_edges(
129	                src=current,
130	                relation=CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON.value,
131	                include_retracted=False,
132	            ):
133	                queue.append(edge.dst)
134

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from collections import deque
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Optional
from uuid import uuid4

from noosphere.models import (
    CascadeEdge,
    CascadeEdgeRelation,
    CascadeNode,
    CascadeNodeKind,
)

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class CascadeCycleError(Exception):
    """Adding this edge would create a cycle in the depends_on subgraph."""


class CascadeGraph:
    def __init__(self, store) -> None:  # noqa: ANN001
        self._store = store

    # ── node helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def add_node(
        self,
        *,
        kind: CascadeNodeKind,
        ref: str,
        attrs: Optional[dict] = None,
        node_id: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> str:
        nid = node_id or str(uuid4())
        node = CascadeNode(
            node_id=nid,
            kind=kind,
            ref=ref,
            attrs=attrs or {},
        )
        self._store.insert_cascade_node(node)
        return nid

    # ── edge helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def add_edge(
        self,
        *,
        src: str,
        dst: str,
        relation: CascadeEdgeRelation,
        method_invocation_id: str,
        confidence: float,
        unresolved: bool = False,
    ) -> str:
        from noosphere.store import CascadeEdgeConflictError, CascadeEdgeOrphanError

        inv = self._store.get_method_invocation(method_invocation_id)
        if inv is None:
            raise CascadeEdgeOrphanError(
                f"method_invocation_id {method_invocation_id!r} not found"
            )

        if relation in (CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS, CascadeEdgeRelation.REFUTES):
            opposite = (
                CascadeEdgeRelation.REFUTES
                if relation == CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS
                else CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS
            )
            for existing in self._store.iter_cascade_edges(
                src=src, dst=dst, relation=opposite.value, include_retracted=False
            ):
                raise CascadeEdgeConflictError(
                    f"Non-retracted {opposite.value} edge exists "
                    f"between {src} -> {dst}"
                )

        if relation == CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON:
            self._check_cycle(src, dst)

        edge_id = str(uuid4())
        edge = CascadeEdge(
            edge_id=edge_id,
            src=src,
            dst=dst,
            relation=relation,
            method_invocation_id=method_invocation_id,
            confidence=confidence,
            unresolved=unresolved,
            established_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
        )
        self._store.insert_cascade_edge(edge)
        return edge_id

    def retract_edge(self, edge_id: str) -> None:
        self._store.retract_cascade_edge(edge_id, datetime.now(timezone.utc))

    def iter_edges(
        self,
        *,
        src: Optional[str] = None,
        dst: Optional[str] = None,
        relation: Optional[str] = None,
        include_retracted: bool = False,
    ):
        yield from self._store.iter_cascade_edges(
            src=src, dst=dst, relation=relation, include_retracted=include_retracted
        )

    # ── cycle detection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _check_cycle(self, src: str, dst: str) -> None:
        """BFS forward from dst in depends_on subgraph; if we reach src it's a cycle."""
        visited: set[str] = set()
        queue: deque[str] = deque([dst])
        while queue:
            current = queue.popleft()
            if current == src:
                raise CascadeCycleError(
                    f"Adding depends_on edge {src} -> {dst} would create a cycle"
                )
            if current in visited:
                continue
            visited.add(current)
            for edge in self._store.iter_cascade_edges(
                src=current,
                relation=CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON.value,
                include_retracted=False,
            ):
                queue.append(edge.dst)

1	"""Curated failure-mode catalogs for registered methods.
2	
3	A failure-mode catalog is a YAML file that sits next to the method
4	implementation (``<method>.FAILURES.yaml``) and lists the ways the
5	method is known or suspected to break. The catalog is human-curated:
6	LLMs may suggest entries, but a human must approve. The loader here is
7	strict — a malformed or missing catalog raises at import time so CI
8	catches drift.
9	
10	Two query paths are exposed:
11	
12	* :func:`failure_modes_for` — given a method name and a conclusion,
13	  filter the catalog to entries whose trigger conditions plausibly
14	  apply. The match is LLM-assisted with a deterministic threshold; the
15	  decision is persisted (input hash → output) so the same conclusion
16	  yields the same matched set without re-paying for inference.
17	* :func:`load_catalog` / :func:`load_all_catalogs` — raw access for
18	  publishing surfaces (founder UI, public methodology page, CLI lint).
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import hashlib
24	import json
25	import os
26	import threading
27	from pathlib import Path
28	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Literal, Optional
29	
30	import yaml
31	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError, field_validator
32	
33	Severity = Literal["low", "medium", "high"]
34	
35	
36	class FailureModeCitation(BaseModel):
37	    """A single citation backing a failure mode."""
38	
39	    title: str = Field(min_length=1)
40	    url: Optional[str] = None
41	    note: Optional[str] = None
42	
43	
44	class FailureMode(BaseModel):
45	    """One curated failure mode for a method."""
46	
47	    name: str = Field(min_length=1)
48	    description: str
49	    worked_example: str = Field(min_length=1)
50	    trigger_conditions: str = Field(min_length=1)
51	    mitigation: str = Field(min_length=1)
52	    severity: Severity
53	    citations: list[FailureModeCitation] = Field(default_factory=list)
54	    public: bool = False
55	
56	    @field_validator("description")
57	    @classmethod
58	    def _description_min_two_sentences(cls, v: str) -> str:
59	        text = v.strip()
60	        if not text:
61	            raise ValueError("description is required")
62	        # A "sentence" here is a span ending in . ! or ? — counted
63	        # liberally so that quoted text and abbreviations don't trip
64	        # us up in the common case.
65	        sentences = [s for s in _split_sentences(text) if s.strip()]
66	        if len(sentences) < 2:
67	            raise ValueError(
68	                "description must have at least two sentences "
69	                "(separate sentences with `.`, `!`, or `?`)"
70	            )
71	        return text
72	
73	
74	def _split_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
75	    out: list[str] = []
76	    buf: list[str] = []
77	    for ch in text:
78	        buf.append(ch)
79	        if ch in ".!?":
80	            out.append("".join(buf).strip())
81	            buf = []
82	    tail = "".join(buf).strip()
83	    if tail:
84	        out.append(tail)
85	    return out
86	
87	
88	class FailureCatalog(BaseModel):
89	    """Top-level shape of a ``<method>.FAILURES.yaml`` file.
90	
91	    Either ``modes`` is a non-empty list of :class:`FailureMode`, or
92	    the catalog is explicitly opted out with ``failures:
93	    deliberately-empty`` and a ≥2-sentence justification.
94	    """
95	
96	    method: str = Field(min_length=1)
97	    failures: Optional[Literal["deliberately-empty"]] = None
98	    justification: Optional[str] = None
99	    modes: list[FailureMode] = Field(default_factory=list)
100	
101	    @classmethod
102	    def validate_shape(cls, data: dict[str, Any]) -> "FailureCatalog":
103	        catalog = cls.model_validate(data)
104	        if catalog.failures == "deliberately-empty":
105	            if catalog.modes:
106	                raise ValueError(
107	                    "deliberately-empty catalog must not list modes"
108	                )
109	            justification = (catalog.justification or "").strip()
110	            sentences = [
111	                s for s in _split_sentences(justification) if s.strip()
112	            ]
113	            if len(sentences) < 2:
114	                raise ValueError(
115	                    "deliberately-empty catalog requires a justification "
116	                    "of at least two sentences"
117	                )
118	        else:
119	            if not catalog.modes:
120	                raise ValueError(
121	                    "catalog must list at least one mode, or set "
122	                    "`failures: deliberately-empty` with a ≥2-sentence "
123	                    "justification"
124	                )
125	        return catalog
126	
127	
128	# ── Loader ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
129	
130	_METHODS_DIR = Path(__file__).parent
131	_CATALOG_SUFFIX = ".FAILURES.yaml"
132	
133	
134	class FailureCatalogError(Exception):
135	    """Raised when a catalog is missing or malformed."""
136	
137	
138	def catalog_path(method_name: str, methods_dir: Optional[Path] = None) -> Path:
139	    return (methods_dir or _METHODS_DIR) / f"{method_name}{_CATALOG_SUFFIX}"
140	
141	
142	def load_catalog(
143	    method_name: str, methods_dir: Optional[Path] = None
144	) -> FailureCatalog:
145	    path = catalog_path(method_name, methods_dir)
146	    if not path.exists():
147	        raise FailureCatalogError(
148	            f"missing failure-mode catalog: {path.name} "
149	            f"(every registered method requires one; opt out with "
150	            f"`failures: deliberately-empty` + justification)"
151	        )
152	    try:
153	        raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
154	    except yaml.YAMLError as exc:
155	        raise FailureCatalogError(f"YAML parse error in {path.name}: {exc}")
156	    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
157	        raise FailureCatalogError(
158	            f"{path.name}: top-level must be a mapping"
159	        )
160	    raw.setdefault("method", method_name)
161	    try:
162	        return FailureCatalog.validate_shape(raw)
163	    except (ValidationError, ValueError) as exc:
164	        raise FailureCatalogError(f"{path.name}: {exc}")
165	
166	
167	def load_all_catalogs(
168	    methods_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
169	) -> dict[str, FailureCatalog]:
170	    base = methods_dir or _METHODS_DIR
171	    out: dict[str, FailureCatalog] = {}
172	    for path in sorted(base.glob(f"*{_CATALOG_SUFFIX}")):
173	        method_name = path.name[: -len(_CATALOG_SUFFIX)]
174	        out[method_name] = load_catalog(method_name, methods_dir=base)
175	    return out
176	
177	
178	# ── LLM-assisted match (with persistent cache) ────────────────────────
179	
180	_DEFAULT_THRESHOLD = 0.5
181	_PROMPT_VERSION = "failure-match/v1"
182	_DEFAULT_CACHE_PATH = (
183	    Path(os.environ.get("NOOSPHERE_DATA_DIR", "noosphere_data"))
184	    / "failure_match_cache.json"
185	)
186	_CACHE_LOCK = threading.Lock()
187	
188	
189	class FailureMatch(BaseModel):
190	    """One mode's match decision for a given conclusion."""
191	
192	    mode_name: str
193	    score: float
194	    matched: bool
195	    rationale: str = ""
196	
197	
198	class FailureMatchResult(BaseModel):
199	    """The full audit record for a (method, conclusion) match call."""
200	
201	    method: str
202	    input_hash: str
203	    model_name: str
204	    prompt_version: str
205	    threshold: float
206	    matches: list[FailureMatch]
207	
208	
209	def _hash_inputs(
210	    method: str,
211	    conclusion_text: str,
212	    mode_names: Iterable[str],
213	    prompt_version: str,
214	    model_name: str,
215	    threshold: float,
216	) -> str:
217	    payload = json.dumps(
218	        {
219	            "method": method,
220	            "conclusion": conclusion_text,
221	            "modes": sorted(mode_names),
222	            "prompt_version": prompt_version,
223	            "model_name": model_name,
224	            "threshold": threshold,
225	        },
226	        sort_keys=True,
227	    )
228	    return hashlib.sha256(payload.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
229	
230	
231	def _load_cache(cache_path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
232	    if not cache_path.exists():
233	        return {}
234	    try:
235	        return json.loads(cache_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
236	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
237	        return {}
238	
239	
240	def _save_cache(cache_path: Path, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
241	    cache_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
242	    tmp = cache_path.with_suffix(cache_path.suffix + ".tmp")
243	    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2, sort_keys=True), encoding="utf-8")
244	    tmp.replace(cache_path)
245	
246	
247	# Default deterministic matcher: lexical overlap between the trigger
248	# conditions and the conclusion text. Production deployments swap this
249	# for an LLM matcher via the ``matcher`` parameter.
250	def _lexical_match_score(trigger: str, conclusion_text: str) -> float:
251	    def _tokens(s: str) -> set[str]:
252	        return {t.lower().strip(".,;:!?") for t in s.split() if len(t) > 3}
253	
254	    a = _tokens(trigger)
255	    b = _tokens(conclusion_text)
256	    if not a:
257	        return 0.0
258	    return len(a & b) / max(1, len(a))
259	
260	
261	Matcher = Callable[[FailureMode, str], tuple[float, str]]
262	
263	
264	def _default_matcher(mode: FailureMode, conclusion_text: str) -> tuple[float, str]:
265	    score = _lexical_match_score(mode.trigger_conditions, conclusion_text)
266	    rationale = (
267	        f"lexical-overlap={score:.3f} between trigger and conclusion"
268	    )
269	    return score, rationale
270	
271	
272	def failure_modes_for(
273	    method: str,
274	    conclusion: Any,
275	    *,
276	    methods_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
277	    matcher: Optional[Matcher] = None,
278	    threshold: float = _DEFAULT_THRESHOLD,
279	    model_name: str = "lexical-overlap-v1",
280	    prompt_version: str = _PROMPT_VERSION,
281	    cache_path: Optional[Path] = None,
282	) -> FailureMatchResult:
283	    """Return failure modes whose trigger conditions plausibly apply.
284	
285	    The match decision is keyed on a SHA-256 of (method, conclusion
286	    text, sorted mode names, prompt version, model, threshold). Repeat
287	    calls with the same inputs read from the on-disk cache and pay no
288	    inference cost.
289	    """
290	
291	    catalog = load_catalog(method, methods_dir=methods_dir)
292	    text = _extract_conclusion_text(conclusion)
293	    matcher_fn = matcher or _default_matcher
294	
295	    if catalog.failures == "deliberately-empty":
296	        return FailureMatchResult(
297	            method=method,
298	            input_hash=_hash_inputs(
299	                method, text, [], prompt_version, model_name, threshold
300	            ),
301	            model_name=model_name,
302	            prompt_version=prompt_version,
303	            threshold=threshold,
304	            matches=[],
305	        )
306	
307	    mode_names = [m.name for m in catalog.modes]
308	    digest = _hash_inputs(
309	        method, text, mode_names, prompt_version, model_name, threshold
310	    )
311	    cache_file = cache_path or _DEFAULT_CACHE_PATH
312	
313	    with _CACHE_LOCK:
314	        cache = _load_cache(cache_file)
315	        cached = cache.get(digest)
316	        if cached is not None:
317	            try:
318	                return FailureMatchResult.model_validate(cached)
319	            except ValidationError:
320	                pass
321	
322	    matches: list[FailureMatch] = []
323	    for mode in catalog.modes:
324	        score, rationale = matcher_fn(mode, text)
325	        score = max(0.0, min(1.0, float(score)))
326	        matches.append(
327	            FailureMatch(
328	                mode_name=mode.name,
329	                score=score,
330	                matched=score >= threshold,
331	                rationale=rationale,
332	            )
333	        )
334	
335	    result = FailureMatchResult(
336	        method=method,
337	        input_hash=digest,
338	        model_name=model_name,
339	        prompt_version=prompt_version,
340	        threshold=threshold,
341	        matches=matches,
342	    )
343	
344	    with _CACHE_LOCK:
345	        cache = _load_cache(cache_file)
346	        cache[digest] = result.model_dump()
347	        _save_cache(cache_file, cache)
348	
349	    return result
350	
351	
352	def matched_modes(
353	    result: FailureMatchResult, catalog: FailureCatalog
354	) -> list[FailureMode]:
355	    """Resolve a match result back to the FailureMode objects it picks."""
356	
357	    by_name = {m.name: m for m in catalog.modes}
358	    return [
359	        by_name[m.mode_name]
360	        for m in result.matches
361	        if m.matched and m.mode_name in by_name
362	    ]
363	
364	
365	def merge_catalogs(
366	    catalogs: Iterable[FailureCatalog],
367	) -> dict[str, list[FailureMode]]:
368	    """Group all modes across catalogs by method name. Useful for
369	    reviewers that want priors across an ensemble of methods."""
370	
371	    out: dict[str, list[FailureMode]] = {}
372	    for cat in catalogs:
373	        if cat.failures == "deliberately-empty":
374	            out.setdefault(cat.method, [])
375	            continue
376	        out.setdefault(cat.method, []).extend(cat.modes)
377	    return out
378	
379	
380	def _extract_conclusion_text(conclusion: Any) -> str:
381	    if conclusion is None:
382	        return ""
383	    if isinstance(conclusion, str):
384	        return conclusion
385	    for attr in ("text", "description", "claim"):
386	        v = getattr(conclusion, attr, None)
387	        if isinstance(v, str) and v:
388	            return v
389	    if isinstance(conclusion, dict):
390	        for key in ("text", "description", "claim"):
391	            v = conclusion.get(key)
392	            if isinstance(v, str) and v:
393	                return v
394	    return str(conclusion)
395	
396	
397	# ── Author-time tooling ────────────────────────────────────────────────
398	
399	_SCAFFOLD_TEMPLATE = """\
400	method: {method}
401	modes:
402	  - name: example_failure_mode
403	    description: |
404	      Replace this with a real description of how the method fails.
405	      Two sentences minimum: name the failure, then say what makes it
406	      hard to detect from inside the method's normal output.
407	    worked_example: |
408	      Cite a real or constructed case where this failure was observed.
409	    trigger_conditions: |
410	      Free-text conditions that should put a reviewer on alert.
411	    mitigation: |
412	      What the firm should do when this trigger fires.
413	    severity: medium
414	    citations: []
415	    public: false
416	"""
417	
418	
419	def scaffold_catalog(method_name: str, methods_dir: Optional[Path] = None) -> Path:
420	    """Write a starter ``<method>.FAILURES.yaml`` if one does not exist."""
421	
422	    path = catalog_path(method_name, methods_dir)
423	    if path.exists():
424	        raise FailureCatalogError(
425	            f"refusing to overwrite existing catalog at {path}"
426	        )
427	    path.write_text(_SCAFFOLD_TEMPLATE.format(method=method_name), encoding="utf-8")
428	    return path
429	
430	
431	def lint_all(methods_dir: Optional[Path] = None) -> dict[str, FailureCatalog]:
432	    """Validate every catalog under ``methods_dir`` and return them.
433	    Raises :class:`FailureCatalogError` on the first problem."""
434	
435	    return load_all_catalogs(methods_dir=methods_dir)
436	
437	
438	__all__ = [
439	    "FailureCatalog",
440	    "FailureCatalogError",
441	    "FailureMatch",
442	    "FailureMatchResult",
443	    "FailureMode",
444	    "FailureModeCitation",
445	    "Matcher",
446	    "catalog_path",
447	    "failure_modes_for",
448	    "lint_all",
449	    "load_all_catalogs",
450	    "load_catalog",
451	    "matched_modes",
452	    "merge_catalogs",
453	    "scaffold_catalog",
454	]
455

"""Curated failure-mode catalogs for registered methods.

A failure-mode catalog is a YAML file that sits next to the method
implementation (``<method>.FAILURES.yaml``) and lists the ways the
method is known or suspected to break. The catalog is human-curated:
LLMs may suggest entries, but a human must approve. The loader here is
strict — a malformed or missing catalog raises at import time so CI
catches drift.

Two query paths are exposed:

* :func:`failure_modes_for` — given a method name and a conclusion,
  filter the catalog to entries whose trigger conditions plausibly
  apply. The match is LLM-assisted with a deterministic threshold; the
  decision is persisted (input hash → output) so the same conclusion
  yields the same matched set without re-paying for inference.
* :func:`load_catalog` / :func:`load_all_catalogs` — raw access for
  publishing surfaces (founder UI, public methodology page, CLI lint).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import os
import threading
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Literal, Optional

import yaml
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError, field_validator

Severity = Literal["low", "medium", "high"]


class FailureModeCitation(BaseModel):
    """A single citation backing a failure mode."""

    title: str = Field(min_length=1)
    url: Optional[str] = None
    note: Optional[str] = None


class FailureMode(BaseModel):
    """One curated failure mode for a method."""

    name: str = Field(min_length=1)
    description: str
    worked_example: str = Field(min_length=1)
    trigger_conditions: str = Field(min_length=1)
    mitigation: str = Field(min_length=1)
    severity: Severity
    citations: list[FailureModeCitation] = Field(default_factory=list)
    public: bool = False

    @field_validator("description")
    @classmethod
    def _description_min_two_sentences(cls, v: str) -> str:
        text = v.strip()
        if not text:
            raise ValueError("description is required")
        # A "sentence" here is a span ending in . ! or ? — counted
        # liberally so that quoted text and abbreviations don't trip
        # us up in the common case.
        sentences = [s for s in _split_sentences(text) if s.strip()]
        if len(sentences) < 2:
            raise ValueError(
                "description must have at least two sentences "
                "(separate sentences with `.`, `!`, or `?`)"
            )
        return text


def _split_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
    out: list[str] = []
    buf: list[str] = []
    for ch in text:
        buf.append(ch)
        if ch in ".!?":
            out.append("".join(buf).strip())
            buf = []
    tail = "".join(buf).strip()
    if tail:
        out.append(tail)
    return out


class FailureCatalog(BaseModel):
    """Top-level shape of a ``<method>.FAILURES.yaml`` file.

    Either ``modes`` is a non-empty list of :class:`FailureMode`, or
    the catalog is explicitly opted out with ``failures:
    deliberately-empty`` and a ≥2-sentence justification.
    """

    method: str = Field(min_length=1)
    failures: Optional[Literal["deliberately-empty"]] = None
    justification: Optional[str] = None
    modes: list[FailureMode] = Field(default_factory=list)

    @classmethod
    def validate_shape(cls, data: dict[str, Any]) -> "FailureCatalog":
        catalog = cls.model_validate(data)
        if catalog.failures == "deliberately-empty":
            if catalog.modes:
                raise ValueError(
                    "deliberately-empty catalog must not list modes"
                )
            justification = (catalog.justification or "").strip()
            sentences = [
                s for s in _split_sentences(justification) if s.strip()
            ]
            if len(sentences) < 2:
                raise ValueError(
                    "deliberately-empty catalog requires a justification "
                    "of at least two sentences"
                )
        else:
            if not catalog.modes:
                raise ValueError(
                    "catalog must list at least one mode, or set "
                    "`failures: deliberately-empty` with a ≥2-sentence "
                    "justification"
                )
        return catalog


# ── Loader ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

_METHODS_DIR = Path(__file__).parent
_CATALOG_SUFFIX = ".FAILURES.yaml"


class FailureCatalogError(Exception):
    """Raised when a catalog is missing or malformed."""


def catalog_path(method_name: str, methods_dir: Optional[Path] = None) -> Path:
    return (methods_dir or _METHODS_DIR) / f"{method_name}{_CATALOG_SUFFIX}"


def load_catalog(
    method_name: str, methods_dir: Optional[Path] = None
) -> FailureCatalog:
    path = catalog_path(method_name, methods_dir)
    if not path.exists():
        raise FailureCatalogError(
            f"missing failure-mode catalog: {path.name} "
            f"(every registered method requires one; opt out with "
            f"`failures: deliberately-empty` + justification)"
        )
    try:
        raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    except yaml.YAMLError as exc:
        raise FailureCatalogError(f"YAML parse error in {path.name}: {exc}")
    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
        raise FailureCatalogError(
            f"{path.name}: top-level must be a mapping"
        )
    raw.setdefault("method", method_name)
    try:
        return FailureCatalog.validate_shape(raw)
    except (ValidationError, ValueError) as exc:
        raise FailureCatalogError(f"{path.name}: {exc}")


def load_all_catalogs(
    methods_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
) -> dict[str, FailureCatalog]:
    base = methods_dir or _METHODS_DIR
    out: dict[str, FailureCatalog] = {}
    for path in sorted(base.glob(f"*{_CATALOG_SUFFIX}")):
        method_name = path.name[: -len(_CATALOG_SUFFIX)]
        out[method_name] = load_catalog(method_name, methods_dir=base)
    return out


# ── LLM-assisted match (with persistent cache) ────────────────────────

_DEFAULT_THRESHOLD = 0.5
_PROMPT_VERSION = "failure-match/v1"
_DEFAULT_CACHE_PATH = (
    Path(os.environ.get("NOOSPHERE_DATA_DIR", "noosphere_data"))
    / "failure_match_cache.json"
)
_CACHE_LOCK = threading.Lock()


class FailureMatch(BaseModel):
    """One mode's match decision for a given conclusion."""

    mode_name: str
    score: float
    matched: bool
    rationale: str = ""


class FailureMatchResult(BaseModel):
    """The full audit record for a (method, conclusion) match call."""

    method: str
    input_hash: str
    model_name: str
    prompt_version: str
    threshold: float
    matches: list[FailureMatch]


def _hash_inputs(
    method: str,
    conclusion_text: str,
    mode_names: Iterable[str],
    prompt_version: str,
    model_name: str,
    threshold: float,
) -> str:
    payload = json.dumps(
        {
            "method": method,
            "conclusion": conclusion_text,
            "modes": sorted(mode_names),
            "prompt_version": prompt_version,
            "model_name": model_name,
            "threshold": threshold,
        },
        sort_keys=True,
    )
    return hashlib.sha256(payload.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()


def _load_cache(cache_path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    if not cache_path.exists():
        return {}
    try:
        return json.loads(cache_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
        return {}


def _save_cache(cache_path: Path, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    cache_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    tmp = cache_path.with_suffix(cache_path.suffix + ".tmp")
    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2, sort_keys=True), encoding="utf-8")
    tmp.replace(cache_path)


# Default deterministic matcher: lexical overlap between the trigger
# conditions and the conclusion text. Production deployments swap this
# for an LLM matcher via the ``matcher`` parameter.
def _lexical_match_score(trigger: str, conclusion_text: str) -> float:
    def _tokens(s: str) -> set[str]:
        return {t.lower().strip(".,;:!?") for t in s.split() if len(t) > 3}

    a = _tokens(trigger)
    b = _tokens(conclusion_text)
    if not a:
        return 0.0
    return len(a & b) / max(1, len(a))


Matcher = Callable[[FailureMode, str], tuple[float, str]]


def _default_matcher(mode: FailureMode, conclusion_text: str) -> tuple[float, str]:
    score = _lexical_match_score(mode.trigger_conditions, conclusion_text)
    rationale = (
        f"lexical-overlap={score:.3f} between trigger and conclusion"
    )
    return score, rationale


def failure_modes_for(
    method: str,
    conclusion: Any,
    *,
    methods_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
    matcher: Optional[Matcher] = None,
    threshold: float = _DEFAULT_THRESHOLD,
    model_name: str = "lexical-overlap-v1",
    prompt_version: str = _PROMPT_VERSION,
    cache_path: Optional[Path] = None,
) -> FailureMatchResult:
    """Return failure modes whose trigger conditions plausibly apply.

    The match decision is keyed on a SHA-256 of (method, conclusion
    text, sorted mode names, prompt version, model, threshold). Repeat
    calls with the same inputs read from the on-disk cache and pay no
    inference cost.
    """

    catalog = load_catalog(method, methods_dir=methods_dir)
    text = _extract_conclusion_text(conclusion)
    matcher_fn = matcher or _default_matcher

    if catalog.failures == "deliberately-empty":
        return FailureMatchResult(
            method=method,
            input_hash=_hash_inputs(
                method, text, [], prompt_version, model_name, threshold
            ),
            model_name=model_name,
            prompt_version=prompt_version,
            threshold=threshold,
            matches=[],
        )

    mode_names = [m.name for m in catalog.modes]
    digest = _hash_inputs(
        method, text, mode_names, prompt_version, model_name, threshold
    )
    cache_file = cache_path or _DEFAULT_CACHE_PATH

    with _CACHE_LOCK:
        cache = _load_cache(cache_file)
        cached = cache.get(digest)
        if cached is not None:
            try:
                return FailureMatchResult.model_validate(cached)
            except ValidationError:
                pass

    matches: list[FailureMatch] = []
    for mode in catalog.modes:
        score, rationale = matcher_fn(mode, text)
        score = max(0.0, min(1.0, float(score)))
        matches.append(
            FailureMatch(
                mode_name=mode.name,
                score=score,
                matched=score >= threshold,
                rationale=rationale,
            )
        )

    result = FailureMatchResult(
        method=method,
        input_hash=digest,
        model_name=model_name,
        prompt_version=prompt_version,
        threshold=threshold,
        matches=matches,
    )

    with _CACHE_LOCK:
        cache = _load_cache(cache_file)
        cache[digest] = result.model_dump()
        _save_cache(cache_file, cache)

    return result


def matched_modes(
    result: FailureMatchResult, catalog: FailureCatalog
) -> list[FailureMode]:
    """Resolve a match result back to the FailureMode objects it picks."""

    by_name = {m.name: m for m in catalog.modes}
    return [
        by_name[m.mode_name]
        for m in result.matches
        if m.matched and m.mode_name in by_name
    ]


def merge_catalogs(
    catalogs: Iterable[FailureCatalog],
) -> dict[str, list[FailureMode]]:
    """Group all modes across catalogs by method name. Useful for
    reviewers that want priors across an ensemble of methods."""

    out: dict[str, list[FailureMode]] = {}
    for cat in catalogs:
        if cat.failures == "deliberately-empty":
            out.setdefault(cat.method, [])
            continue
        out.setdefault(cat.method, []).extend(cat.modes)
    return out


def _extract_conclusion_text(conclusion: Any) -> str:
    if conclusion is None:
        return ""
    if isinstance(conclusion, str):
        return conclusion
    for attr in ("text", "description", "claim"):
        v = getattr(conclusion, attr, None)
        if isinstance(v, str) and v:
            return v
    if isinstance(conclusion, dict):
        for key in ("text", "description", "claim"):
            v = conclusion.get(key)
            if isinstance(v, str) and v:
                return v
    return str(conclusion)


# ── Author-time tooling ────────────────────────────────────────────────

_SCAFFOLD_TEMPLATE = """\
method: {method}
modes:
  - name: example_failure_mode
    description: |
      Replace this with a real description of how the method fails.
      Two sentences minimum: name the failure, then say what makes it
      hard to detect from inside the method's normal output.
    worked_example: |
      Cite a real or constructed case where this failure was observed.
    trigger_conditions: |
      Free-text conditions that should put a reviewer on alert.
    mitigation: |
      What the firm should do when this trigger fires.
    severity: medium
    citations: []
    public: false
"""


def scaffold_catalog(method_name: str, methods_dir: Optional[Path] = None) -> Path:
    """Write a starter ``<method>.FAILURES.yaml`` if one does not exist."""

    path = catalog_path(method_name, methods_dir)
    if path.exists():
        raise FailureCatalogError(
            f"refusing to overwrite existing catalog at {path}"
        )
    path.write_text(_SCAFFOLD_TEMPLATE.format(method=method_name), encoding="utf-8")
    return path


def lint_all(methods_dir: Optional[Path] = None) -> dict[str, FailureCatalog]:
    """Validate every catalog under ``methods_dir`` and return them.
    Raises :class:`FailureCatalogError` on the first problem."""

    return load_all_catalogs(methods_dir=methods_dir)


__all__ = [
    "FailureCatalog",
    "FailureCatalogError",
    "FailureMatch",
    "FailureMatchResult",
    "FailureMode",
    "FailureModeCitation",
    "Matcher",
    "catalog_path",
    "failure_modes_for",
    "lint_all",
    "load_all_catalogs",
    "load_catalog",
    "matched_modes",
    "merge_catalogs",
    "scaffold_catalog",
]

1	"""Method drift estimator.
2	
3	A method's track record is a *running* phenomenon. Aggregating all of its
4	resolved forecasts into a single calibration slope (which is what
5	`method_track_record.py` produces) is the right thing to publish on the
6	"how does this method do, in total?" panel. It is the wrong thing to
7	watch for the question "is the method getting worse *right now*?" — by
8	the time enough bad calls accumulate to drag the lifetime slope below
9	some threshold, a quarter of the firm's output has already shipped on a
10	broken instrument.
11	
12	This module computes per-method, per-window calibration statistics over
13	rolling time windows (defaults: 30 / 90 / 180 days), compares each
14	window's calibration slope and Brier mean to the method's *trailing
15	baseline* (median of prior windows), and produces a drift p-value via
16	permutation test. Parametric z-tests would be wrong here: at n=8..40
17	resolutions per window the binary outcome distribution does not satisfy
18	OLS variance assumptions tightly enough to trust the analytic SE.
19	
20	Three constraints the tests pin:
21	
22	* **No drift alert is allowed for n < 8 resolutions in the window.** The
23	  estimator returns `WindowStat(insufficient=True, ...)` instead.
24	* **Permutation tests use a fixed seed.** The seed is recorded on the
25	  emitted DriftEvent so a reviewer can re-run the test bit-for-bit.
26	* **Lineage breaks are honored.** Callers may pass an `earliest_eligible`
27	  cutoff per method (Conclusion Lineage retire/revive event) and the
28	  estimator drops resolutions whose source predictions were observed
29	  before that cutoff. There is no implicit smoothing across regime
30	  breaks the firm has manually flagged.
31	
32	This module deliberately does no I/O. The scheduler in
33	`scheduler_drift.py` pulls rows, calls `evaluate_method`, and persists
34	DriftEvents. Tests exercise this module directly with synthetic rows.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import math
40	import random
41	import statistics
42	import uuid
43	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
44	from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
45	from typing import Iterable, Optional, Sequence
46	
47	
48	METHOD_DRIFT_SCHEMA = "theseus.method_drift.v1"
49	DEFAULT_WINDOW_DAYS: tuple[int, ...] = (30, 90, 180)
50	DEFAULT_PERMUTATION_ITERATIONS = 500
51	DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_ITERATIONS = 400
52	MIN_WINDOW_SAMPLE = 8
53	DEFAULT_SEED = 0xD71F70FF
54	WARN_SIGMA = 1.5
55	ESCALATE_SIGMA = 2.0
56	HYSTERESIS_CLEAN_WINDOWS = 2
57	
58	
59	# ── Inputs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
60	
61	
62	@dataclass(frozen=True)
63	class DriftResolution:
64	    """One resolution feeding the drift estimator.
65	
66	    `observed_at` is the time the prediction was *made* (or otherwise
67	    became active in the world), NOT the time the market settled — drift
68	    is about when the method made the call, not when the call paid out.
69	    """
70	
71	    prediction_id: str
72	    probability: float
73	    outcome: float  # 0.0 or 1.0
74	    observed_at: datetime
75	    brier: Optional[float] = None
76	    domain: str = ""
77	
78	
79	# ── Outputs ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
80	
81	
82	@dataclass(frozen=True)
83	class WindowStat:
84	    """Calibration metrics for a single (method, window) pair."""
85	
86	    window_days: int
87	    window_start: datetime
88	    window_end: datetime
89	    sample_size: int
90	    calibration_slope: Optional[float]
91	    brier_mean: Optional[float]
92	    directional_bias: Optional[float]  # mean(outcome - probability); >0 under-confident
93	    insufficient: bool = False
94	
95	
96	@dataclass(frozen=True)
97	class DriftAssessment:
98	    """One window's assessment relative to the method's trailing baseline."""
99	
100	    window: WindowStat
101	    baseline_slope: Optional[float]
102	    baseline_brier: Optional[float]
103	    slope_delta: Optional[float]
104	    brier_delta: Optional[float]
105	    p_value: Optional[float]
106	    sigma: Optional[float]
107	    severity: str  # "ok" | "warn" | "escalate" | "insufficient"
108	    seed: int
109	
110	
111	@dataclass
112	class DriftEventRecord:
113	    """Materialized drift event ready to be persisted (DB-agnostic)."""
114	
115	    id: str
116	    organization_id: str
117	    method_name: str
118	    method_version: str
119	    domain: str
120	    observed_at: datetime
121	    window_days: int
122	    severity: str
123	    sigma: Optional[float]
124	    p_value: Optional[float]
125	    sample_size: int
126	    calibration_slope: Optional[float]
127	    baseline_slope: Optional[float]
128	    brier_mean: Optional[float]
129	    baseline_brier: Optional[float]
130	    directional_bias: Optional[float]
131	    seed: int
132	    notes: str = ""
133	
134	    def evidence(self) -> dict[str, object]:
135	        return {
136	            "schema": METHOD_DRIFT_SCHEMA,
137	            "method_name": self.method_name,
138	            "method_version": self.method_version,
139	            "domain": self.domain,
140	            "window_days": self.window_days,
141	            "severity": self.severity,
142	            "sigma": self.sigma,
143	            "p_value": self.p_value,
144	            "sample_size": self.sample_size,
145	            "calibration_slope": self.calibration_slope,
146	            "baseline_slope": self.baseline_slope,
147	            "brier_mean": self.brier_mean,
148	            "baseline_brier": self.baseline_brier,
149	            "directional_bias": self.directional_bias,
150	            "seed": self.seed,
151	        }
152	
153	
154	# ── Statistics ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
155	
156	
157	def _ols_slope(rows: Sequence[DriftResolution]) -> Optional[float]:
158	    if len(rows) < 2:
159	        return None
160	    n = len(rows)
161	    mean_x = sum(r.probability for r in rows) / n
162	    mean_y = sum(r.outcome for r in rows) / n
163	    num = 0.0
164	    den = 0.0
165	    for r in rows:
166	        dx = r.probability - mean_x
167	        num += dx * (r.outcome - mean_y)
168	        den += dx * dx
169	    if den <= 0.0:
170	        return None
171	    return num / den
172	
173	
174	def _brier_mean(rows: Sequence[DriftResolution]) -> Optional[float]:
175	    vals = [r.brier if r.brier is not None else (r.probability - r.outcome) ** 2 for r in rows]
176	    if not vals:
177	        return None
178	    return sum(vals) / len(vals)
179	
180	
181	def _directional_bias(rows: Sequence[DriftResolution]) -> Optional[float]:
182	    if not rows:
183	        return None
184	    return sum(r.outcome - r.probability for r in rows) / len(rows)
185	
186	
187	def window_stats(
188	    rows: Sequence[DriftResolution],
189	    *,
190	    window_days: int,
191	    end: datetime,
192	) -> WindowStat:
193	    """Compute statistics for one rolling window ending at `end`.
194	
195	    Rows are filtered to those whose `observed_at` falls in
196	    [end - window_days, end). The window is inclusive on the start side
197	    (so a row at exactly the lower boundary is in) and exclusive on the
198	    end side (so two adjacent windows do not double-count the same row).
199	    """
200	    start = end - timedelta(days=window_days)
201	    in_window = [
202	        r for r in rows
203	        if _ensure_tz(r.observed_at) >= _ensure_tz(start)
204	        and _ensure_tz(r.observed_at) < _ensure_tz(end)
205	    ]
206	    n = len(in_window)
207	    if n < MIN_WINDOW_SAMPLE:
208	        return WindowStat(
209	            window_days=window_days,
210	            window_start=start,
211	            window_end=end,
212	            sample_size=n,
213	            calibration_slope=None,
214	            brier_mean=None,
215	            directional_bias=None,
216	            insufficient=True,
217	        )
218	    return WindowStat(
219	        window_days=window_days,
220	        window_start=start,
221	        window_end=end,
222	        sample_size=n,
223	        calibration_slope=_ols_slope(in_window),
224	        brier_mean=_brier_mean(in_window),
225	        directional_bias=_directional_bias(in_window),
226	        insufficient=False,
227	    )
228	
229	
230	def _trailing_baseline(prior: Sequence[WindowStat]) -> tuple[Optional[float], Optional[float], Optional[float]]:
231	    """Median + MAD of prior windows' slope and Brier mean.
232	
233	    Returns (median_slope, median_brier, mad_slope).
234	    Drops insufficient windows. Returns Nones when there are fewer than
235	    two usable prior windows — drift cannot be assessed against a
236	    trailing baseline of one point.
237	    """
238	    usable = [w for w in prior if not w.insufficient and w.calibration_slope is not None]
239	    if len(usable) < 2:
240	        return (None, None, None)
241	    slopes = [w.calibration_slope for w in usable if w.calibration_slope is not None]
242	    briers = [w.brier_mean for w in usable if w.brier_mean is not None]
243	    median_slope = statistics.median(slopes) if slopes else None
244	    median_brier = statistics.median(briers) if briers else None
245	    if median_slope is None:
246	        return (None, median_brier, None)
247	    abs_dev = [abs(s - median_slope) for s in slopes]
248	    mad = statistics.median(abs_dev) if abs_dev else 0.0
249	    # Convert MAD to a comparable σ (Gaussian consistency factor 1.4826).
250	    sigma_like = mad * 1.4826 if mad > 0 else 0.0
251	    return (median_slope, median_brier, sigma_like)
252	
253	
254	def permutation_p_value(
255	    current: Sequence[DriftResolution],
256	    prior: Sequence[DriftResolution],
257	    *,
258	    iterations: int = DEFAULT_PERMUTATION_ITERATIONS,
259	    seed: int = DEFAULT_SEED,
260	) -> Optional[float]:
261	    """Two-sided permutation test on the difference of OLS slopes between
262	    the current window and the union of prior windows.
263	
264	    Returns None if either bucket has fewer than `MIN_WINDOW_SAMPLE`
265	    rows or if the slope is undefined on the observed split (zero
266	    variance in probabilities).
267	    """
268	    if len(current) < MIN_WINDOW_SAMPLE or len(prior) < MIN_WINDOW_SAMPLE:
269	        return None
270	    s_current = _ols_slope(current)
271	    s_prior = _ols_slope(prior)
272	    if s_current is None or s_prior is None:
273	        return None
274	    observed = abs(s_current - s_prior)
275	    pooled = list(current) + list(prior)
276	    n_current = len(current)
277	    rng = random.Random(seed)
278	    extreme = 0
279	    valid = 0
280	    for _ in range(max(1, iterations)):
281	        rng.shuffle(pooled)
282	        a = pooled[:n_current]
283	        b = pooled[n_current:]
284	        sa = _ols_slope(a)
285	        sb = _ols_slope(b)
286	        if sa is None or sb is None:
287	            continue
288	        valid += 1
289	        if abs(sa - sb) >= observed - 1e-12:
290	            extreme += 1
291	    if valid < iterations // 2:
292	        # Too many degenerate resamples — treat as undefined.
293	        return None
294	    # +1 smoothing so an exact match cannot return p=0.
295	    return (extreme + 1) / (valid + 1)
296	
297	
298	# ── Top-level evaluation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
299	
300	
301	def evaluate_method(
302	    *,
303	    organization_id: str,
304	    method_name: str,
305	    method_version: str,
306	    rows: Sequence[DriftResolution],
307	    as_of: datetime,
308	    domain: str = "",
309	    window_days: Sequence[int] = DEFAULT_WINDOW_DAYS,
310	    earliest_eligible: Optional[datetime] = None,
311	    seed: int = DEFAULT_SEED,
312	    permutation_iterations: int = DEFAULT_PERMUTATION_ITERATIONS,
313	    prior_window_count: int = 4,
314	) -> list[DriftAssessment]:
315	    """Assess drift for one (method, version, domain) at `as_of`.
316	
317	    For each `window_days` in the input, compute the current window's
318	    statistics, build a trailing baseline of `prior_window_count`
319	    non-overlapping prior windows of the same length, and emit a
320	    `DriftAssessment`.
321	
322	    Lineage handling: rows whose `observed_at` precedes
323	    `earliest_eligible` are dropped before any windowing. This is the
324	    sole hook for excluding pre-revival data from a retire-and-revive
325	    method; it must be passed in by the scheduler from a
326	    ConclusionLineage row when present.
327	    """
328	    rows = [
329	        r for r in rows
330	        if r.domain == domain
331	        and (earliest_eligible is None or _ensure_tz(r.observed_at) >= _ensure_tz(earliest_eligible))
332	    ]
333	    out: list[DriftAssessment] = []
334	    for w in window_days:
335	        current = window_stats(rows, window_days=w, end=as_of)
336	        # Build prior windows: each of `w` days, immediately preceding,
337	        # non-overlapping. Used both for the trailing baseline and the
338	        # permutation pool.
339	        prior_stats: list[WindowStat] = []
340	        prior_rows: list[DriftResolution] = []
341	        for k in range(1, prior_window_count + 1):
342	            prior_end = current.window_start - timedelta(days=w * (k - 1))
343	            ws = window_stats(rows, window_days=w, end=prior_end)
344	            prior_stats.append(ws)
345	            if not ws.insufficient:
346	                prior_rows.extend(
347	                    r for r in rows
348	                    if _ensure_tz(r.observed_at) >= _ensure_tz(ws.window_start)
349	                    and _ensure_tz(r.observed_at) < _ensure_tz(ws.window_end)
350	                )
351	
352	        baseline_slope, baseline_brier, sigma_like = _trailing_baseline(prior_stats)
353	
354	        if current.insufficient:
355	            out.append(
356	                DriftAssessment(
357	                    window=current,
358	                    baseline_slope=baseline_slope,
359	                    baseline_brier=baseline_brier,
360	                    slope_delta=None,
361	                    brier_delta=None,
362	                    p_value=None,
363	                    sigma=None,
364	                    severity="insufficient",
365	                    seed=seed,
366	                )
367	            )
368	            continue
369	
370	        slope_delta = (
371	            current.calibration_slope - baseline_slope
372	            if (current.calibration_slope is not None and baseline_slope is not None)
373	            else None
374	        )
375	        brier_delta = (
376	            current.brier_mean - baseline_brier
377	            if (current.brier_mean is not None and baseline_brier is not None)
378	            else None
379	        )
380	
381	        # σ score: how far from the baseline in MAD-derived units.
382	        # We score the *direction that is bad*: slope going DOWN, Brier
383	        # going UP. A baseline with zero MAD (all priors identical) is
384	        # treated as no σ available — we cannot scale a deviation
385	        # against zero spread, and forcing infinity would swamp the
386	        # alert policy.
387	        sigma: Optional[float] = None
388	        if slope_delta is not None and sigma_like and sigma_like > 0:
389	            # Negative slope_delta = degradation; flip sign so positive σ
390	            # means "drifting worse".
391	            sigma = max(0.0, -slope_delta / sigma_like)
392	
393	        # Current rows for the permutation test.
394	        current_rows = [
395	            r for r in rows
396	            if _ensure_tz(r.observed_at) >= _ensure_tz(current.window_start)
397	            and _ensure_tz(r.observed_at) < _ensure_tz(current.window_end)
398	        ]
399	        p_value = permutation_p_value(
400	            current_rows,
401	            prior_rows,
402	            iterations=permutation_iterations,
403	            seed=seed,
404	        )
405	
406	        severity = _classify(sigma, p_value)
407	        out.append(
408	            DriftAssessment(
409	                window=current,
410	                baseline_slope=baseline_slope,
411	                baseline_brier=baseline_brier,
412	                slope_delta=slope_delta,
413	                brier_delta=brier_delta,
414	                p_value=p_value,
415	                sigma=sigma,
416	                severity=severity,
417	                seed=seed,
418	            )
419	        )
420	    return out
421	
422	
423	def _classify(sigma: Optional[float], p_value: Optional[float]) -> str:
424	    """Map (σ, p) → severity label.
425	
426	    Rules:
427	    * σ ≥ ESCALATE_SIGMA AND p ≤ 0.05 → "escalate"
428	    * σ ≥ WARN_SIGMA  AND p ≤ 0.10 → "warn"
429	    * otherwise → "ok"
430	    σ alone or p alone is not enough; both have to fire so a thin
431	    baseline (small MAD) cannot escalate by itself.
432	    """
433	    if sigma is None or p_value is None:
434	        return "ok"
435	    if sigma >= ESCALATE_SIGMA and p_value <= 0.05:
436	        return "escalate"
437	    if sigma >= WARN_SIGMA and p_value <= 0.10:
438	        return "warn"
439	    return "ok"
440	
441	
442	def assessment_to_event(
443	    assessment: DriftAssessment,
444	    *,
445	    organization_id: str,
446	    method_name: str,
447	    method_version: str,
448	    domain: str,
449	    observed_at: Optional[datetime] = None,
450	) -> DriftEventRecord:
451	    """Materialize an assessment as a DriftEventRecord ready to persist.
452	
453	    The id is deterministic on (organization, method, version, domain,
454	    window, window_end) so re-running the scheduler over the same
455	    window does not create a duplicate row — the scheduler upserts on
456	    this id."""
457	    obs = observed_at or assessment.window.window_end
458	    deterministic_seed = "|".join(
459	        [
460	            organization_id,
461	            method_name,
462	            method_version,
463	            domain,
464	            str(assessment.window.window_days),
465	            assessment.window.window_end.isoformat(),
466	        ]
467	    )
468	    event_id = "drift_" + uuid.uuid5(uuid.NAMESPACE_URL, deterministic_seed).hex[:24]
469	    return DriftEventRecord(
470	        id=event_id,
471	        organization_id=organization_id,
472	        method_name=method_name,
473	        method_version=method_version,
474	        domain=domain,
475	        observed_at=obs,
476	        window_days=assessment.window.window_days,
477	        severity=assessment.severity,
478	        sigma=assessment.sigma,
479	        p_value=assessment.p_value,
480	        sample_size=assessment.window.sample_size,
481	        calibration_slope=assessment.window.calibration_slope,
482	        baseline_slope=assessment.baseline_slope,
483	        brier_mean=assessment.window.brier_mean,
484	        baseline_brier=assessment.baseline_brier,
485	        directional_bias=assessment.window.directional_bias,
486	        seed=assessment.seed,
487	    )
488	
489	
490	# ── Helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
491	
492	
493	def _ensure_tz(dt: datetime) -> datetime:
494	    if dt.tzinfo is None:
495	        return dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
496	    return dt
497	
498	
499	__all__ = [
500	    "DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_ITERATIONS",
501	    "DEFAULT_PERMUTATION_ITERATIONS",
502	    "DEFAULT_SEED",
503	    "DEFAULT_WINDOW_DAYS",
504	    "ESCALATE_SIGMA",
505	    "HYSTERESIS_CLEAN_WINDOWS",
506	    "METHOD_DRIFT_SCHEMA",
507	    "MIN_WINDOW_SAMPLE",
508	    "WARN_SIGMA",
509	    "DriftAssessment",
510	    "DriftEventRecord",
511	    "DriftResolution",
512	    "WindowStat",
513	    "assessment_to_event",
514	    "evaluate_method",
515	    "permutation_p_value",
516	    "window_stats",
517	]
518
